title: Noun なしには～ない
titleEn: Means without, cannot do without, not possible without
explanation: Dùng để nói rằng nếu không có Noun thì điều phía sau không thể xảy ra. Mẫu này nhấn mạnh tính không thể thiếu của Noun trong điều kiện hoặc kết quả. Cấu trúc: Noun + なしには + Verb-negative.
explanationEn: The なしには～ない grammar point is used to express that without the mentioned noun, something is not possible. It can be translated as cannot (do) without , or not possible without
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun ならいざ知らず
titleEn: Expresses maybe X is possible, but Y is definitely not. Often let alone
structure: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment)
structureEn: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment)
explanation: Dùng để nói “nếu là Noun thì còn hiểu được, nhưng trường hợp này thì không”. Mẫu này thường dùng để phê bình hoặc nhấn mạnh sự không phù hợp. Cấu trúc: Noun1 + ならいざ知らず + Noun2 (comment).
explanationEn: 「Xならいざ知らず、Yは...」 emphasizes that while X might be possible or acceptable, Y is out of the question or even more difficult. It can be translated as I could understand X, but Y is impossible or Maybe X, but definitely not Y
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun + ならでは
titleEn: Expresses something unique or characteristic only of that noun; only found in, only possible with
explanation: Dùng để nói một đặc điểm, giá trị hoặc khả năng chỉ có ở Noun. Có thể hiểu là “chỉ Noun mới có”, “đúng chất Noun”. Cấu trúc: Noun + ならでは.
explanationEn: The pattern Noun + ならでは highlights something that is unique or distinctive to that noun, suggesting that only this place, person, or thing could possess or create it
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun なり Noun なり
titleEn: Lists alternatives of the same kind; either A or B, A or B, for example
structure: Noun + なり + Noun + なり
structureEn: Noun + なり + Noun + なり
explanation: Dùng để nêu vài lựa chọn cùng loại và gợi ý người nghe chọn một trong số đó. Sắc thái là “A hay B chẳng hạn”. Cấu trúc: Noun + なり + Noun + なり.
explanationEn: The pattern AなりBなり is used to suggest alternatives or options, usually of the same category. It can be translated as whether A or B or A or B, for example
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun なりとも
titleEn: Indicates even a small amount or degree; at least, even a little
explanation: Dùng để nói “dù chỉ là Noun cũng được”, nhấn mạnh một mức tối thiểu hoặc một lượng nhỏ vẫn có giá trị. Cấu trúc: Noun + なりとも.
explanationEn: Noun + なりとも is used to show that even a small amount or degree is valuable or needed. It translates as at least or even a little in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun に Noun を重ねて
titleEn: This expression is used to describe repeated actions or states, one after another or pile up
structure: Noun に Noun を重ねて
structureEn: Noun に Noun を重ねて
explanation: Dùng để diễn tả hành động hoặc trạng thái lặp đi lặp lại, chồng chất theo thời gian. Thường dùng với các danh từ như 努力, 練習, 失敗. Cấu trúc: Noun に Noun を重ねて.
explanationEn: The structure Noun に Noun を重ねて is used to indicate the repetition of actions or states. In English, this could be translated as one after another or pile up. Its typically used to emphasize a great deal of continuation or accumulation of something
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun にあっては
titleEn: Indicates a specific situation or environment in which something happens or is the case
explanation: Dùng để nêu một hoàn cảnh, môi trường hoặc lập trường đặc biệt, trong đó điều phía sau xảy ra hoặc được xem là đúng. Sắc thái trang trọng, văn viết. Cấu trúc: Noun + にあっては.
explanationEn: The にあっては grammar point is used after a noun that indicates a specific situation or environment. It expresses the context in which an event takes place, or the circumstances that pertain to the information to be explained or highlighted. It can be translated as in, at or under in English
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun にあるまじき Noun
titleEn: Expresses the idea of unfitting, unbecoming or inappropriate
structure: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2
structureEn: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2
explanation: Dùng để nói một hành vi hoặc thái độ không phù hợp với vị trí, tư cách hoặc vai trò Noun. Thường mang sắc thái phê phán mạnh. Cấu trúc: Noun1 にあるまじき Noun2.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun にあるまじき Noun is used to express that the noun doesnt fit or is inappropriate in some way for the other noun. It can translate loosely as unfitting for, unbecoming of or something that must not be for a/ the noun
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)

title: Noun にして
titleEn: Expresses the idea even for, even though, or in spite of
explanation: Dùng để nhấn mạnh “ngay cả ở mức Noun mà...” hoặc “chỉ đến Noun mới...”, tùy ngữ cảnh. Mẫu này trang trọng và thường dùng để đánh giá mức độ đặc biệt. Cấu trúc: Noun + にして.
explanationEn: The grammar point Noun にして expresses a sense of surprise, disbelief or unexpectedness in response to a situation or circumstance. Generally, it is used when the speaker wants to convey that something is unexpected even for someone (noun) or in their situation
editorialSources: Hanabira grammar JSON (source import)
JLPT Vietnam / JLPT Sensei Vietnam (manual QA reference only)
JLPT Global / JLPT Sensei / Practice Japanese (manual QA reference only)
